Assembly: The assembly idBleFall4.1 is based on 56x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using gEVAL. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size.

Taxonomy_y: Eukaryota; Metazoa; Ecdysozoa; Arthropoda; Hexapoda; Insecta; Pterygota; Neoptera; Endopterygota; Lepidoptera; Glossata; Ditrysia; Tortricoidea; Tortricidae; Olethreutinae; Grapholitini; Dichrorampha
Assembly: The assembly ilDicObsc2.hap1.1 is based on 38x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements (~https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).The order and orientation of scaffolds in the following locations is uncertainChromosome 11 between approximatlee 34.9Mb to the endChromosome W between approximately 5.7-40.3Mb.

Assembly: The assembly ilSatPrun1.hap1.1 is based on 26x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Chromosome Z was assigned by synteny to GCA_963565745.1.

Assembly: The assembly ilAplPlag1.hap1.1 is based on 124x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Darwin Tree of Life Project~(https://www.darwintreeoflife.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on coverage and BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements~(https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4). This species appears to exhibit the ZO karyotype as there was no evidence of a W chromosome in the assembly.

Assembly: The assembly jhHydViri1.1 is based on 529x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Aquatic Symbiosis Genomics Project (https://www.aquaticsymbiosisgenomics.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm, retained haplotig separation with purge_dups, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, the primary assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 5 (722 Kbp - 1.03 Mbp), chromosome 9 (6.45 - 6.76 Mbp), chromosome 10 (6.54 - 6.86 Mbp) and chromosome 11 (4.43 - 4.71 Mbp) are unknown.

Assembly: The assembly fThuThy2.1 is based on 37x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by the Wellcome Sanger Institute Tree of Life Programme (https://www.sanger.ac.uk/programme/tree-of-life/) as part of the European Reference Genome Atlas pilot project~(https://www.erga-biodiversity.eu/pilot-project). Assembly: The assembly ilIdaFili5.hap1.1 is based on 33x PacBio data and Arima2 Hi-C data generated by Project Psyche (https://www.projectpsyche.org/). The assembly process included the following sequence of steps: initial PacBio assembly generation with Hifiasm in Hi-C integrated assembly mode, and Hi-C based scaffolding with YaHS. The mitochondrial genome was assembled using MitoHiFi. Finally, each haplotype assembly was analysed and manually improved using TreeVal. Chromosome-scale scaffolds confirmed by the Hi-C data have been named in order of size. Z chromosome identified based on BUSCO gene painting with ancestral Merian elements ( https://doi.org/10.1038/s41559-024-02329-4).The Z and W chromosomes were identified based on its single-copy status within a diploid assembly. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome W (1,333 - 10,097 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 13 (16,202 - 16,662 Kbp) are unknown. The exact order and orientation of the contigs on chromosome 30 (4,826 - 5,317 Kbp) are unknown. A haplotypic inversion was observed in the region on chromosome 28 (3,547- 4,506 bp).No BUSCO genes were detected on chromosomes 28 and 32. Further investigation is required to determine the nature of these chromosomes, but confirmation is not possible with the current data. Chromosome 32 may represent a B-like chromosome. On chromosome 28, the region spanning positions 5,728?9,839 is present as a single copy in the hap1 assembly, resulting in approximately half the PacBio coverage, similar to that observed for the sex chromosomes. This pattern is unusual, and additional data will be required to confirm the identity and biological significance of these chromosomes.
